skill.name: run-tests
skill.description: Run project tests and guide focused verification after code changes.
skill.summary: Provides concise test commands for full, targeted, changed, and coverage runs.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: The external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs agents to run Bash test commands. The weak cryptography findings are false positives; no cryptographic code or algorithm selection appears in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External Test Commands Require Trusted Workspaces
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to run npm test commands and targeted test commands through Bash. This is expected for a test skill, but project test scripts can execute arbitrary local code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash command examples directly invoke npm test. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it confirms external command execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 33 do not show cryptographic code, hashing, encryption, or algorithm selection. Line 3 is metadata, and line 33 is a test naming example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no weak cryptographic primitive or crypto API. The analyzer appears to have matched unrelated text.

low_findings.title: Inline Test Pattern Misread As Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static alerts at SKILL.md lines 32 and 33 point to Markdown inline examples. They describe test file globs and JavaScript test naming, not shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is clearly inside Markdown list items, not a Bash block. No command interpreter would execute these examples directly.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Test Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains Bash examples for full test runs, targeted file tests, changed tests, and coverage. These commands can trigger project-defined npm scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in fenced Bash blocks and are part of the intended workflow. The risk depends on the repository being tested.

content.user_title: Run Project Tests With AI Guidance
content.value_statement: Agents need clear test commands before changing code. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concise workflow for full, targeted, changed, and coverage tests.
actual_capabilities: Shows a default command for running all project tests.
Shows a targeted test command using a file placeholder.
Shows a command for changed tests since HEAD.
Provides a short checklist for debugging failed tests.
limitations: Assumes an npm-based test workflow by default.
Does not detect the active framework automatically.
Does not install dependencies or configure test tools.
Bash commands can execute project-defined scripts in the workspace.

use_cases.title: Verify a Code Change
use_cases.description: Run the standard project tests after editing source files.

use_cases.title: Check One Test File
use_cases.description: Run focused tests for a file related to the current change.

use_cases.title: Review Coverage Before Merge
use_cases.description: Run coverage tests and inspect failures before opening a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Run All Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-tests skill to run the full project test suite and summarize the result.

prompt_templates.title: Run Tests For One File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-tests skill to test the file I changed and report any failures.

prompt_templates.title: Run Changed Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-tests skill to run tests affected by changes since HEAD.

prompt_templates.title: Verify With Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-tests skill to run coverage, analyze failures, and list follow-up fixes.

output_examples.output: The agent runs the default test command, reports pass or fail status, and highlights failing test names.

output_examples.input: Test only the file I edited.
output_examples.output: The agent runs the targeted test workflow and summarizes errors from the related test file.
The agent runs coverage tests and identifies failing areas that need attention.

best_practices: Run tests only in repositories you trust.
Start with targeted tests before running the full suite.
Review failed test output before changing source code.
anti_patterns: Do not run project scripts from unknown repositories without review.
Do not assume npm commands match every project.
Do not ignore failures from changed or targeted tests.

faq.question: Which tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides commands and guidance. The agent still executes commands in the active workspace.

faq.question: What test framework is assumed?
faq.answer: The examples use npm test. Comments mention pytest, Go tests, and Cargo tests as alternatives.

faq.question: Can it run coverage?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a coverage command for npm-based projects.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted code?
faq.answer: Use caution. Test commands can run project-defined scripts and should be limited to trusted repositories.

faq.question: Does it debug failures?
faq.answer: It gives a short checklist for reading errors, checking files, and isolating failed tests.
